name: Hawaii Kai
desc: Mainland-feeling master-planned suburb — marinas, Costco, safe, clean, family-oriented, but limited restaurants.
insider: Koko Marina Center has Kona Brewing Company's Oahu taproom — grab a Longboard Lager and watch paddleboarders on the marina at sunset
Hanauma Bay is literally in your backyard, and residents with a Hawaii ID skip the $25 entry fee — go at 6:45am Wednesday through Sunday before crowds descend
The Costco here is one of the busiest in the entire country — avoid weekends completely and go Tuesday or Wednesday mornings before 10am
Maunalua Bay has free kayak and paddleboard launching — paddle east along the coast to see reef, turtles, and the China Walls cliff formations
Koko Head Crater Railway Trail (1,048 railroad ties straight up) is the East Side's most brutal workout — locals do it at 5am with headlamps
Kokonuts Shave Ice at Koko Marina is the locals' pick over chain shops — the haupia cream and fresh lilikoi are outstanding
Sandy Beach is beautiful to watch but has one of the highest lifeguard rescue rates on Oahu — never bodyboard here without experience and never turn your back on the shore break
Hawaii Kai Towne Center has Zippy's, Longs, and local plate lunch spots — it's the everyday hub, while Koko Marina is more the weekend hangout side
The Hawaii Kai commute into town via Kalanianaole Highway is reliably 35-50 minutes during morning rush — there is literally only one road in and out
The marina-side townhomes along Lunalilo Home Road offer the most affordable waterfront living on Oahu's south shore, often under $700K
